reasoning: Magnus Carlsen has maintained the FIDE world #1 ranking continuously since 2011. Going into June 2025, his official FIDE rating on the Wikipedia list is 2837, which gives him a staggering 33-point lead over the world #2, Hikaru Nakamura (2804). The first resolution date (June 15) relies on the already-published June list, making his #1 ranking virtually guaranteed (0.98), barring a bizarre Wikipedia formatting anomaly. For the July 8 resolution, the list will include recent Norway Chess 2025 results; Carlsen notably won this tournament stringing together excellent performances (a 3020 performance rating with the White pieces) ensuring that his rating remains comfortably ahead of the pack (0.97). While his participation in subsequent fall/winter classical events introduces slight downside risk for September and December, bridging a >30-point rating chasm within six months requires a historically anomalous collapse by Carlsen combined with an unprecedented hot streak from a rival. Furthermore, FIDE inactivity rules only remove players after 12 months, and his recent participation in Norway Chess strictly avoids this until mid-2026. Therefore, probabilities decrease only marginally over the forecast horizon to account for unforeseen shocks.
